mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment rises during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and real GDP confirms broad-based aggregate demand growth, supporting corporate earnings revisions and driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and GDPC1 fails to produce positive 60-day returns in over 60% of observations, or interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates capacity constraints and rising input costs, compressing profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth yields positive 20-day returns in late-cycle regimes, or the negative correlation reverses when capacity utilization remains below 78%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, raising discount rates and causing immediate negative equity price adjustments within five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal is neutralized by concurrent fiscal stimulus announcements.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in over 55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when wage growth exceeds 3.5% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in housing starts reflect strong household balance sheets and construction sector activity, forecasting higher corporate revenues and positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows, or housing starts growth fails to predict 60-day returns when mortgage rates exceed 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising federal funds rates signals a supply-demand mismatch during monetary tightening, forcing immediate risk premium repricing and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of tightening periods, or the interaction term loses predictive power when global commodity prices fall simultaneously.
